name: FM Broadcast
description: Commercial FM radio stations

description: VHF Omnidirectional Range and Instrument Landing Systems

name: Aviation Voice (AM)
description: Air traffic control and pilot communications

name: Aviation Emergency
description: International aviation distress frequency — Guard channel

description: NOAA-18 weather satellite APT image downlink

description: NOAA-19 weather satellite APT image downlink

description: NOAA-15 weather satellite APT image downlink

description: Russian Meteor-M LRPT weather satellite downlink

name: 2m Amateur / APRS
description: Ham radio voice; 144.390 is North American APRS

name: ISS Voice Downlink
description: International Space Station voice downlink (when active)

name: Marine VHF / AIS
description: Maritime communications; 161.975 & 162.025 are AIS

description: International maritime distress and calling frequency

name: NOAA Weather Radio
description: US National Weather Service continuous broadcasts

name: ELT / EPIRB Distress
description: 406 MHz emergency locator transmitter activations

name: ISM 433 MHz
description: European ISM band: weather stations, car fobs, sensors

name: 70cm Amateur / CubeSats
description: Ham radio; many CubeSat and amateur satellite downlinks

name: ISS APRS Digipeater
description: International Space Station APRS position/packet relay

name: FRS/GMRS (US walkie)
description: Family Radio Service and General Mobile Radio Service

name: ISM 915 MHz / LoRa
description: US ISM band: LoRa gateways, smart meters, Z-Wave

name: ADS-B (aircraft)
description: 1090 MHz ADS-B aircraft transponders (position, speed)

name: Inmarsat / Iridium
description: Satellite phone and data signals (L-band)
